title: Conditional formatting should be set for all users of shared boards
excerpt:
Why is conditional formatting applied to individual views only instead of all users of a shared board? 
Our specific use case is setting row colours to dates within “this week”, and dates older than this week. It a great…

posts.author: kirstenD (Kirsten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Why is conditional formatting applied to individual views only instead of all users of a shared board?

Our specific use case is setting row colours to dates within “this week”, and dates older than this week. It a great visual that quickly highlights tasks to focus on, as well as tasks that have not been updated by a due date.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: I have conditional formatting on boards and the other users DO see it. Can you clarify what condition settings you’re using?

posts.author: kirstenD (Kirsten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Desiree,

Thank you for replying. This is what I’ve set, and it is a shared board where all members have permission to edit. Unfortunately, not one member (other than myself) is able to see it. Is there another method for setting for all users?

image788×252 13.4 KB
